answer:
The integral \( G(s) = \int_0^\infty e^{-st} \, dt \) converges when the exponent \(-st\) has a negative real part, which requires \(\operatorname{Re}(s) > 0\), and evaluates to \(1/s\). This matches the standard Laplace transform of \(f(t)=1\), which is \(1/s\) for \(\operatorname{Re}(s) > 0\). Therefore, the correct choice is C.

\boxed{C}
